city_report.transport: Buses leave from outside Arrivals every 15-30 min and take 15 min to go downtown. Simplest is to buy a €1 single ticket by card, and you might pay extra for bulky luggage. "Get around" below describes other ticket deals, such as the smart card Vilniečio kortelė — buy it from the Narvesen kiosk next to Arrivals.

attraction.name: Gediminas Tower
attraction.description: The only remaining tower of the Upper Castle.

attraction.name: Uzupis
attraction.description: Bohemian district that declared its own republic.

attraction.name: Vilnius Cathedral
attraction.description: Main Roman Catholic cathedral of Lithuania.

attraction.name: Gates of Dawn
attraction.description: The only surviving gate of the city's original walls.

attraction.name: Grand Dukes Palace
attraction.description: Reconstructed palace of the Lithuanian rulers.

attraction.name: Bernadine Garden
attraction.description: Main park in the heart of the old town.

attraction.name: Church of St. Peter
attraction.description: Baroque church known for its white plaster carvings.

attraction.name: Three Crosses
attraction.description: Monument on the hill offering panoramic views.

attraction.name: Museum of Occupations
attraction.description: Former KGB headquarters now a museum.

city_report.description: Capitale verdeggiante e accogliente, Vilnius si distingue per un centro storico barocco e una politica di incentivi alle startup. Offre una qualità della vita elevata con costi decisamente contenuti.
city_report.wiki_intro: Vilnius is the capital and largest city of Lithuania. It has a beautiful baroque Old Town, listed as a , and excellent tourist facilities in all price ranges. It stands on the River Neris in the southeast pocket of the country, the Dzūkija region, and in 2023 had a population of 629,000. Vilnius has been central to the country's life for as long as there has been Lithuania, but that has often...
